howItWorks.intro: This cycling power calculator uses standard formulas to provide accurate results.
rules: Training zones based on percentage of FTP
howItWorks.outro: Individual results vary based on metabolism, genetics, and activity level. Consult your doctor before starting any program.

faq.question: What is FTP?
faq.answer: Functional Threshold Power — the maximum average power you can sustain for one hour. It is the gold standard for cycling fitness and training zone calculation.

faq.question: What is a good W/kg?
faq.answer: Recreational: 2-3 W/kg. Cat 3 racer: 3.5-4. Cat 1: 4.5-5. Tour de France riders: 6+ W/kg. Weight matters as much as raw watts.

faq.question: How do I test FTP?
faq.answer: 20-minute all-out effort on a trainer or flat road. FTP = average power × 0.95. Or use a ramp test in Zwift/TrainerRoad.

faq.question: How do I improve FTP?
faq.answer: Sweet spot training (88-94% of FTP) for 20-60 minutes. Threshold intervals at 95-105% for 8-20 minutes. Consistent training over months.

faq.question: Is the Cycling Power Calculator free to use?
faq.answer: Yes, completely free with no signup required. Use it as many times as you need — there are no limits or hidden fees.

sections.title: What Is the Cycling Power Calculator?
sections.body: The Cycling Power Calculator is a free online tool designed for athletes and fitness enthusiasts who need quick, accurate calculations in the fitness and training space. By entering your ftp, weight, you get instant results including watts per kg, zone 2, zone 4. No formulas to memorize, no spreadsheets to build — just enter your numbers and get the answer in seconds. Whether you're a beginner or experienced professional, this calculator saves you time and eliminates guesswork.

sections.title: Why This Calculation Matters
sections.body: Getting watts per kg right can make the difference between success and costly mistakes. In fitness and training, small errors compound quickly. Manual calculations are error-prone and time-consuming, especially under pressure. This calculator applies proven formulas used by athletes and fitness enthusiasts worldwide, giving you confidence that your numbers are correct. Use it to optimize your training with precision and avoid common pitfalls that trip up beginners.

sections.title: When Should You Use This Calculator?
sections.body: This tool is most useful when you know your ftp and need to find the right watts per kg. It's also great for quick estimates before committing to a decision, and to double-check manual calculations or professional quotes, and when comparing different scenarios side by side. Bookmark this page and come back whenever you need a fast, reliable answer — the calculator is always free and requires no signup.

howToSteps.title: Enter Your FTP (watts)
howToSteps.description: Type or select your ftp in the field provided. Use the most accurate value available for best results.

howToSteps.title: Enter Your Weight (lbs)
howToSteps.description: Type or select your weight in the field provided. Use the most accurate value available for best results.

howToSteps.title: Click Calculate
howToSteps.description: Hit the Calculate button to run the numbers. Results appear instantly below.

howToSteps.title: Review Your Results
howToSteps.description: Check your watts per kg, zone 2, zone 4. Use these figures to inform your next decision or compare against alternative scenarios.

tips: Double-check your ftp before calculating — even small input errors can significantly change your results.
